must_detect.matched_text: To: Board of Solaria Berhad (Main Market issuer); From: Legal & Compliance; Re: Project Nangka proposed 22% acquisition of Aurora Plastics Bhd and RM180,000,000 term financing.
Internal research note indicates 6–8% EPS accretion in FY2027 and RM28–32m annual synergies; this forecast is management-only and not in the public domain.

STRICTLY PRIVATE & CONFIDENTIAL — NON-PUBLIC (CMSA) — Embargoed until 30 July 2026, 18:00 MYT.
To: Board of Solaria Berhad (Main Market issuer); From: Legal & Compliance; Re: Project Nangka proposed 22% acquisition of Aurora Plastics Bhd and RM180,000,000 term financing.
Definitive agreements: Share Purchase Agreement with Meranti Capital Sdn. Bhd. and RM180m 5-year Facility Agreement with Bank Bintang Timur Berhad targeted signing 2 Aug 2026; long-stop date 31 Oct 2026.
Conditions precedent include Chapter 10 shareholder approval, Exchange clearance for draft announcement, and no Material Adverse Effect on Aurora Plastics as defined in the SPA.
Negated MAC: normalised raw-material price volatility and any decline in the Main Market index shall not constitute a MAC/MAE for purposes of termination.
Pricing: facility at BR + 3.25% per annum with 12-month interest-only period; security package limited to target shares and assignment of proceeds.
Internal research note indicates 6–8% EPS accretion in FY2027 and RM28–32m annual synergies; this forecast is management-only and not in the public domain.
Draft investor-relations Q&A and immediate announcement are prepared for pre-open release on 31 July 2026, subject to embargo; IR contact Puan Lina Hassan, ir@solaria-berhad.my.
Public-source reference: the industry capacity figures cited are from the 2025 Nusantara Polymers Council report and our Q4 FY2024 production data is already in the published Annual Report 2024.
PDPA notice: this memo uses business contact details only; any personal data of employees will be processed per internal PDPA policy—queries to dpo@solaria-berhad.my.
Board action requested: authorise Dato’ Amir Rahman (CEO) to execute the definitive agreements and to submit the draft announcement to the Exchange.
Insider status: all recipients are considered insiders under the CMSA; do not trade in Solaria Berhad securities until the embargo is lifted and the announcement is released.
Deal checklist excerpt (internal): regulatory filing pack, auditor comfort letter, vendor undertakings, FX hedging term sheet, and integration plan approved by HR and IT.
lowercase project management: chase clean redlines to bank counsel, lock signatory schedule, prep slides, book the war room, and the team spa day is pencilled for 12 aug at 5pm.
Please note the Q1 FY2025 sales figures mentioned in yesterday’s press article are stale and not indicative of current run-rate.
For legal queries contact Ms. Nur Aisha Karim, external counsel at Tualang Law (nur.aisha@tualanglaw.com); for compliance matters reach legal@solaria-berhad.my.
